This script calculates descriptive statistics (mean ± SEM, median, SD, n) and assesses the normality of the distribution (Shapiro-Wilk test, histogram, QQ-plot) of the resting membrane potential (Vm) and input resistance (Ri) recorded from patched endothelia in OHSC.

# load data
df <- read.csv("Endothelia_Resting_Membrane_Potential.csv")

# descriptive statistics
statistics <- function(data, label, unit) {
  data   <- na.omit(data)
  n      <- length(data)
  sd_val <- sd(data)
  sw     <- shapiro.test(data)
  
  cat(sprintf("\n%s (n = %d)\n", label, n))
  cat(sprintf("Mean ± SEM = %.2f ± %.2f %s\n", mean(data), sd_val / sqrt(n), unit))
  cat(sprintf("Median     = %.1f %s\n",         median(data), unit))
  cat(sprintf("SD         = %.2f %s\n",         sd_val, unit))
  cat(sprintf("Shapiro-Wilk: W = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", sw$statistic, sw$p.value))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  
  hist(data, freq = FALSE, breaks = 10, col = "steelblue", border = "white",
       main = paste(label, "— Histogram"),
       xlab = paste0(label, " (", unit, ")"), ylab = "Density")
  qqnorm(data, main = paste(label, "— QQ-Plot"))
  qqline(data, col = "red")
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

invisible(Map(statistics,
              list(df$I.0.Median, df$Ri),
              label = c("Vm", "Ri"),
              unit  = c("mV", "MΩ")))
